title: Pan-Seared Mexican Duck Confit de Pato Ramekin
description: A rich and tangy Mexican-inspired dish featuring tender duck meat, crispy skin, and a hint of citrus.
instructions: Season the duck legs with Mexican spices, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and braise the duck legs for 2 hours.
Remove the duck legs from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Shred the duck meat into bite-sized pieces using two forks.
Heat duck sauce in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the shredded duck meat to the saucepan and stir to coat with the sauce.
Serve the duck meat in ramekins, garnished with chopped cilantro.
Drizzle additional duck sauce over the top, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
